svg.title: How klemr works — your shipment's journey, step by step
svg.desc: Customer-facing infographic showing the seven stages of a shipment lifecycle, with klemr's role described in plain language. Stage 1 explicitly calls out scale photos and item photos as distinct evidence types.
svg.text: How klemr works
your shipment's journey, and what we're doing at every step
When you print the label
klemr captures the dossier — weight, dimensions, address, service, what
you paid — plus photos of the package on the scale and each item inside.
→ Every photo answers a different carrier objection before it can be raised.
When the carrier picks it up
klemr seals your dossier as a tamper-proof record the moment the carrier
takes possession — and matches their first scan to what you shipped.
→ Any mismatch — weight, service, anything — gets flagged the moment they pick up.
While it's in transit
klemr follows every carrier scan from pickup to delivery, and pulls
weather along the route in real time.
→ If your package is late, we already know whether weather was the reason.
When the carrier's bill arrives
klemr checks every line of every invoice — comparing what was charged
against what should have been charged. Overcharges show up as anomalies.
→ Carrier invoices have hundreds of line items. No human reads them all. We do.
We file the dispute for you
When we find an overcharge, klemr files the dispute on your behalf —
with the evidence packet already attached.
→ You don't have to lift a finger.
If the carrier pushes back
Carriers deny disputes for predictable reasons. klemr has a counter
ready for each one — and refiles with stronger evidence.
→ The first denial is almost never the final answer.
When you get paid back
klemr matches the credit to the original claim, confirms you got the
full amount, and flags any short-pays. You see it all on a clean report.
→ You only pay klemr when you get paid. The math reconciles to the dollar.
klemr handles every dispute, every dollar, every receipt.
No setup work. No claim forms. No carrier negotiations. You only pay when you get paid.
